purpose: Define a research-claim proof packet: the durable, independently re-checkable justification for one bounded research claim, joining source refs, an extracted statement, prover and checker attempts, a required negative fixture, verification checks, and a re-derivable verdict by reference and embedded digest rather than by uncited assertion. A failed or unverifiable run still produces a valid packet: a negative result is evidence, not a discarded run.

sources_checked.name: project-telos.science-research-adapters/v1
sources_checked.role: supplies the lawful preprint, literature, and open-access source adapters a claim is extracted from, plus the source-lead non-evidentiary boundary

sources_checked.name: gather provenance receipt
sources_checked.role: supplies the source-ref shape joined by id and content hash; the source body is embedded so its digest is recomputable

sources_checked.name: crucible verdict
sources_checked.role: supplies the check-to-measurement mapping (pass to deviation 0, fail to deviation 1, unverifiable to deviation null) that a check status is derived from, joined by check name

sources_checked.name: project-telos.context-envelope/v1
sources_checked.role: supplies the optional context-ref shape joined by envelope id and content hash

sources_checked.name: research-claim-proof-packet/v0
sources_checked.role: the external export target the exporter conforms to; validated read-only against its ROOT_FIELDS, never imported or spawned at runtime

contract.verification_required: Every packet verdict is MATCH, DRIFT, or UNVERIFIABLE. PROMOTED_DISCOVERY is never assertable in a single packet: it needs independent review.

steps: an archive, preprint, or college source is resolved through a lawful science adapter
one bounded claim is extracted from the source (not a grand theorem, not a clinical result)
a negative fixture is written: a control variant the claim must survive, with a recorded control outcome
prover and checker attempts run and are recorded, positive or negative
each verification check is derived from a crucible measurement and resolved to its declared source refs
the overall verdict is derived by folding the per-check statuses; a single packet reaches at most CRUCIBLE_MATCH, never PROMOTED_DISCOVERY

control_outcomes.note: the recorded outcome of running the claim's check against the negative fixture. survived means the check correctly rejected the control (evidence the check discriminates); not_survived means the check accepted the control (the check does not discriminate and the claim is not established).

promotions.note: the single-packet promotion rungs, aligned with research-claim-proof-packet/v0 PROMOTIONS minus the reproduction-gated rungs. PROMOTED_DISCOVERY is never assertable here.

assembler.accepts: a conformance fixture (happy_path or an operator-supplied fixture)
assembler.returns: a canonical research proof packet plus packet_hash over the hash scope

verifier.returns: named check results, a typed failure list, and a derived verdict MATCH, DRIFT, or UNVERIFIABLE

exporter.returns: a proof-surface research-claim-proof-packet/v0 shaped object whose decision_summary is derived from the overall verdict

gather_provenance.note: each source ref carries the gather receipt ref and the content hash of the embedded body; the verifier recomputes the digest from the body and reports DRIFT on mismatch

crucible_verdict.note: a check status is the crucible measurement verdict for that checker (pass to deviation 0, fail to deviation 1, unverifiable to deviation null, fail-closed)

context_envelope.note: optional; when present, each context ref joins a context envelope by id and content hash

export_contract.decision_summary_source: derived from the packet overall verdict, not from any attempt result or promotion field

promotion_map.note: the internal promotion carries through unchanged; the reserved rungs are never emitted by the exporter

check_status_map.note: internal check status to proof-surface CHECK_STATUSES

per_check_verdict_map.note: internal check status to proof-surface per_check OVERALL_VERDICTS

decision_summary_map.note: proof-surface overall verdict to decision_summary, from _decision.derive_decision_summary

objective.claim: The sum of the first n odd positive integers equals n squared for every n from 1 through 64.
objective.scope: one bounded arithmetic identity checked exhaustively over the stated finite range, not a general theorem over all n
objective.statement: For n in 1..64, 1 + 3 + 5 + ... + (2n - 1) equals n*n. The identity is checked by direct summation at every n in the range.

source_refs.body: Extracted claim: the partial sums of the odd positive integers are the perfect squares. Stated for the finite range n=1..64 so it is checkable by exhaustive summation. Source lead resolved through the open-access literature adapter; the note itself carries the arithmetic definition, so the check does not depend on retrieving the full text.
Check procedure: for each n in 1..64, compute S(n) = sum of (2k-1) for k=1..n by direct addition, and compare S(n) to n*n. The claim passes only if S(n) equals n*n at every n in the range.

negative_fixture.description: A control the claim must survive: the false variant that the sum of the first n odd integers equals n squared plus one. The check must reject this variant. If the check accepted it, the check would not discriminate and the claim would not be established.
negative_fixture.body: Control variant: assert that 1 + 3 + 5 + ... + (2n - 1) equals n*n + 1 for n in 1..64. Run the same direct-summation check against this variant. The variant is false at every n, so a discriminating check reports fail and the control is survived.

attempts.method: direct summation over n=1..64 comparing S(n) to n*n
attempts.notes: S(n) equals n*n at every n in the range; no counterexample in the bounded interval.

evidence: S(n) recomputed by direct addition equals n*n for all n in 1..64
the off-by-one control variant reports fail at every n, so the negative fixture is survived

uncertainty: the identity is verified only over the finite range n=1..64; nothing is claimed for n outside the range
source bodies are embedded and digest-bound but the upstream full texts are not re-fetched during verification

tampered_source_body.mutate: change source_refs[0].body after the content_hash was pinned

check_unresolvable_source_ref.mutate: set checks[0].source_ref_ids to include an id that is not declared in source_refs

embedded_canned_match.mutate: tamper a source body but embed verdicts.overall MATCH
